answer: The corpus contains Tarana-e-Milli as chapter 23 of Bang-E-Dara Part 3 (work slug: allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8) in three variants (original Urdu, transliteration, and English translation). Having read the complete poem in all variants, there is no line in which Iqbal explicitly calls the Muslim community a 'single body' (ek jism / ek tan or equivalent). The poem's unity theme is carried by lines such as 'Muslim hain hum, watan hai saara jahan hamara' (We are Muslims, the whole world is our homeland) and 'Tauheed ki amanat seenon mein hai hamare' (The trust of One God fills our hearts), but none of these amount to calling the ummah a single body. The specific line asked about does not appear in the corpus text of Tarana-e-Milli.
